properties.description: uri for specifications that this metadata record conforms to. Minimimally should specify uri for CDIF discovery profile.

A descriptive name of a dataset (e.g., 'Snow depth in Northern Hemisphere'). The name should uniquely identify the described resource for human use, in the scope of the metadata catalog containing this metadata record.

The primary identifier for the dataset; other identifiers should be listed in the sameAs field. Schema.org has three ways of encoding identifiers-- a text description, a URL, or by using the schema:PropertyValue field. The Science on Schema.org guidance strongly recommends using the PropertyValue approach. see https://github.com/ESIPFed/science-on-schema.org   .... Dataset.md#identifier.  Ideally, for any given data provided they would provide identifiers either all as strings or all as identifier_type.
ISO8601 formatted date (and optional time if relevant) when Dataset was last updated

text statement of conditions for use and access; if an online resource documents the restrictions or a URI is used to identify the conditions, recommend using schema:CreativeWork to provide a label (name) and an identifier (URI or URL).

legal statement of conditions for use and access; recommend using schema:CreativeWork to provide a label (name) for the license, and an identifier. Sources of license identifiers: https://opensource.org/licenses/, https://creativecommons.org/about/cclicenses/, https://spdx.org/licenses/, http://cor.esipfed.org/ont/earthcube/swl. If only a string is provided, it should be an identifier for the license, ideally a resolvable URI

Web Location of a page describing the dataset (landing page), typically providing links or instructions to get the actual resource content; analogous to dcat:accessURL. If a direct link is available to get the data, put in distribution/contentUrl

specifies how to download the data in a specific format or access via a web API. This property describes where to get the data and in what format by using the schema:DataDownload type. If user must access data through a landing page, provide link to landing page in the 'url' property for the dataset
additional type assertions for the catalog record node. dcat:CatalogRecord is required.

iSamples Registrant. identification of the agent that registered the sample, with contact information. Should include person name and affiliation, or position name and affiliation, or just organization name. e-mail address is preferred contact information.
date of most recent update to the metadata content, extends iSamples schema
identify the data collection or repository that contains the described dataset. The value is expected to be a schema:DataCatalog
locator to access a landing page for the collection or catalog
identifier for the collection or catalog; use identifier_type to provide information on identifier scheme and context for identifier

Array of used entities. Must contain an instrument wrapper with schema:hasPart sub-components. The x-ray source type and probe, and monochromator properties type, d-spacing and reflection plane are required.
Human-readable name for the activity
Text description of what this activity did
Formal identifier for this activity
Primary responsible agent for this activity (maps to PROV wasAssociatedWith)
Other participants in this activity
Input entity for this activity (for action chaining, references the result of a prior activity)
Output entity produced by this activity (for action chaining, can be referenced as object of a subsequent activity)
Status of this activity
ISO8601 date-time when the activity started (maps to PROV startedAtTime)
ISO8601 date-time when the activity ended (maps to PROV endedAtTime)
Where the activity occurred
Methodology or protocol for this activity
Error description for failed activities
Domain-specific extension properties
Sub-components of this instrument system. Each item is either an inline instrument description or an @id reference.
Component type classification.
Require additional properties for monochromator, requires d-space, crystal type, reflection plane.
identifier or name for the property concept quantified by the values in this variable slot. Multiple values can specify the property at different levels of granularity.
extend base definition (AdditionalProperty) with some expected propertyID values from NEXUS and XDI specs added in the enum
Description of this distribution, e.g. what format, access constraints, etc.
MIME type with extension; should indicate the serialization scheme in sufficient detail that machine can know how to parse.
A string value calculated from the content of the resource representation, used to test if content has been modified. The checksum is a property of a particular distribution/DataDownload.
An identifier for a standard or specification that the distribution conforms to. Recommended to enable machine-actionable data access.
Party who maintains this particular distribution option for the dataset. Use this property if there are multiple distributions from different providers
Require DefinedTerms for both: absorption edge (XDI dict) and target element (SWEET).
text label for the term that is useful to human user
Identifier for the controlled vocabulary responsible for this keyword.
A representative code for this keyword in the controlled vocabulary (Optional).  Analogous to skos:Notation
extends base CDIF keyword schema to require defined terms for the absorption edge and the target element for the analysis
need to include this to tag what the keyword is about; we're using the keywords as soft-typed properties
other identifiers for the person
restrict to email only. Schema.org allows telephone and postal contacts as well
if affiliation is present, value must be a schema:Organization.
other identifiers for the organization
identifier for organization
other labels by which the organization might be known
string label for organization that is meaningful for human users
text label for the term that is useful to human user
Identifier for the controlled vocabulary responsible for this keyword.
A representative code for this keyword in the controlled vocabulary (Optional).  Analogous to skos:Notation
other labels by which the person might be known
identifier for person, recommend ORCID
string label for person that is meaningful for human users
Catalog record metadata-about-metadata for this instrument description.
Schema or profile this metadata record conforms to.
Date this metadata record was last modified (ISO 8601).
Links to related resources (manuals, datasheets, calibration records, related instruments).
Date the instrument was decommissioned (ISO 8601). Absent if still operational.
Date the instrument was commissioned (ISO 8601).
Instrument-specific properties (measured variables, detection limits, calibration information, operating parameters, etc.).
identifier or name for the property concept quantified by the values in this variable slot. Multiple values can specify the property at different levels of granularity.
Agents associated with this instrument in specific roles (e.g. operator, custodian, principal investigator). Use schema:owner for the owning organization.
Organization that owns this instrument.
Instrument type classification(s) from a controlled vocabulary.
Product model information for this instrument.
Model identifier.
Model name or number.
Organization that manufactured this instrument.
Landing page URL for this instrument.
Formal identifier(s) for this instrument. Use PropertyValue for structured identifiers (e.g. PIDINST, serial number, inventory number).
Alternate name(s), e.g. specific make/model designation, common abbreviation.
Text description of this instrument, its purpose, and capabilities.
Human-readable name for this instrument.
Domain-specific type classifications. Should include a scientific instrument type URI (e.g. wd:Q3099911 for Wikidata scientific instrument).

Optional geographic extent using wkt geometry, see Ocean InfoHub (https://book.oceaninfohub.org/thematics/spatial/README.html#simple-geosparql-wkt). Other geometry schemes might be specified in a specific domain profile, e.g. for atmospheric, subsurface data, or local coordinate systems. NOTE that the location specified here should be the same as the schema.org point or contained within the specified bounding box.
Either a bounding box or a point location.

A GeoShape box defines a curvilinear geometry as a string of latitude-longitude coordinates. A space should be used to separate the latitude and longitude values. The coordinate point pairs are also separated by a space. 'East longitude' means positive longitude values are east of the prime (Greenwich) meridian. e.g. '39.33 120.77 40.44 123.96 41.00 121.34'

A GeoShape box defines an area on the surface of the earth defined by point locations of the southwest corner and northeast corner of the rectangle in latitude-longitude coordinates. A space should be used to separate the latitude and longitude values. The two corner coordinate points are separated by a space. 'East longitude' means positive longitude values are east of the prime (Greenwich) meridian.
multiple place names or DefinedTerms that have a place name and URI for the location
Ordered steps in this methodology
Ordinal position of this step
URL to documentation for this step
Description of what this step involves
Name of this step
URL to a published methodology or protocol document
Description of the methodology
Name of the methodology or protocol
web-resolveable string for the identifier; host name part is location of a resolver that will return some representation for the given identifier value. E.g. https://doi.org/10.5066/F7VX0DMQ
the identifier string. E.g. 10.5066/F7VX0DMQ
In this context for the schema:PropertyValue, this field is an identifier for the identifier schema, e.g. DOI, ARK.  Get values from https://registry.identifiers.org/registry/ for interoperability
